title: Mercimek Corbasi Red Lentil Soup

seo.title: Turkish Red Lentil Soup (Mercimek Corbasi) Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Discover how to make authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup (Mercimek Corbasi) with our easy recipe. A comforting, flavorful, and healthy soup perfect for any meal.

seo.imageAlt: A warm bowl of Turkish Red Lentil Soup (Mercimek Corbasi) garnished with fresh mint and chili flakes.
seo.imageCaption: Savor the rich, comforting flavors of traditional Turkish Red Lentil Soup, known as Mercimek Corbasi.

description: Mercimek Corbasi is a beloved Turkish red lentil soup, cherished for its comforting warmth and rich, earthy flavors. This simple yet deeply satisfying dish is a staple in Turkish homes, often served as a hearty appetizer or a light main course. Its velvety texture and aromatic spices make it an irresistible culinary experience.

instructions.title: Prepare the Lentils
instructions.text: Rinse the red lentils thoroughly under cold running water until the water runs clear. Drain them well and set aside. This helps remove any impurities and starch.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics
instructions.text: In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and carrot. Sauté for 5-7 minutes until the vegetables soften and the onion becomes translucent.

instructions.title: Add Garlic and Tomato Paste
instructions.text: Stir in the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant. Add the tomato paste and cook for 2 minutes, stirring constantly, allowing it to deepen in color and flavor.

instructions.title: Combine and Simmer
instructions.text: Add the rinsed lentils, vegetable broth or water, salt, and black pepper to the pot. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are very tender and have broken down.

instructions.title: Blend the Soup
instructions.text: Remove the pot from the heat. Using an immersion blender, carefully blend the soup until it is smooth and creamy. If you dont have an immersion blender, transfer the soup in batches to a regular blender, being cautious with hot liquids, and blend until smooth. Return the blended soup to the pot.

instructions.title: Prepare the Mint Butter
instructions.text: In a small pan, melt the remaining 1 tablespoon of olive oil over low heat. Add the dried mint and red pepper flakes (Pul Biber). Sauté for about 30 seconds until fragrant, being careful not to burn the spices. This is the traditional topping for Mercimek Corbasi.

instructions.title: Serve
instructions.text: Ladle the hot Mercimek Corbasi into bowls. Drizzle each serving with a spoonful of the aromatic mint butter. Serve immediately with fresh lemon wedges on the side for squeezing, as the lemon brightens the soup's flavor beautifully.

tips: For an extra smooth soup, after blending, you can pass the soup through a fine-mesh sieve, though it is not strictly necessary for traditional Mercimek Corbasi.
The quality of the tomato paste is crucial; use a rich, concentrated paste for the best flavor. Turkish tomato paste is often very flavorful.
Always serve with fresh lemon wedges. Squeezing a bit of lemon juice into the soup before eating adds a vital tangy brightness that complements the earthy lentils beautifully.
article:
Embark on a culinary journey to the heart of Turkey with Mercimek Corbasi, a truly iconic red lentil soup that embodies warmth, comfort, and incredible flavor. This vibrant, creamy soup is more than just a dish; it's a staple in Turkish households, gracing tables from bustling breakfast spreads to elegant evening meals. Known for its deceptively simple ingredients and profound taste, Mercimek Corbasi offers a soul-satisfying experience that is both nourishing and utterly delicious. Whether you're seeking a hearty starter, a light lunch, or a comforting dinner, this traditional Turkish Red Lentil Soup is a masterpiece of culinary simplicity, promising to transport your taste buds straight to the sun-drenched landscapes of Anatolia. Get ready to discover the secrets behind one of Turkey's most cherished culinary treasures.

## What is Mercimek Corbasi Red Lentil Soup?

Mercimek Corbasi, pronounced "mer-jee-mek chor-ba-suh," is a quintessential Turkish soup, celebrated for its rich, earthy flavor and velvety smooth texture. At its heart, Mercimek Corbasi is a creamy, comforting blend of red lentils, often brightened with tomato paste and infused with aromatic spices like dried mint and red pepper flakes. Unlike some lentil soups that might be chunky, the hallmark of a traditional Mercimek Corbasi is its silky consistency, achieved by blending the cooked lentils until perfectly smooth.

This soup holds a special place in Turkish culture. It's often the first course at almost any meal, from humble family dinners to grand feasts. Its versatility and widespread appeal stem from its accessibility – red lentils are inexpensive and readily available – and its ability to provide substantial nourishment. It’s a dish that crosses all social strata, enjoyed by everyone from students to sultans. The vibrant orange-red hue of the soup is as inviting as its aroma, promising a bowlful of warmth and satisfaction. It's naturally gluten-free and can easily be made vegan, making it a beloved option for a wide range of dietary preferences.

## Ingredients You Need

The beauty of Mercimek Corbasi lies in its humble yet powerful ingredient list. Each component plays a crucial role in creating its signature taste and texture.

*   **Red Lentils:** The star of the show. Choose good quality, split red lentils as they cook quickly and break down easily to create the creamy texture.
*   **Onion & Garlic:** The aromatic foundation. Finely diced onion and minced garlic are sautéed to build a flavorful base.
*   **Tomato Paste (Salça):** Essential for depth of flavor and that characteristic reddish hue. Turkish tomato paste is often richer, but any good quality paste will work.
*   **Butter or Olive Oil:** Used for sautéing the aromatics and, traditionally, butter is used to create the fragrant "nane yagi" (mint butter) topping.
*   **Dried Mint (Kuru Nane):** A non-negotiable spice that imparts a unique, refreshing, and distinctly Turkish flavor.
*   **Red Pepper Flakes (Pul Biber):** Adds a gentle warmth and a beautiful visual element. Adjust to your spice preference.
*   **Water or Vegetable/Chicken Broth:** The liquid base. Broth will add more depth, but water works perfectly fine.
*   **Salt & Black Pepper:** To season and enhance all the flavors.
*   **Optional Garnishes:** Fresh lemon wedges (absolutely essential for serving!), a drizzle of chili-infused olive oil, or extra dried mint.

The magic of this Turkish Red Lentil Soup lies in its simplicity and the quality of these core ingredients.

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Creating a delicious Mercimek Corbasi is a straightforward process, even for novice cooks. Follow these steps to achieve a truly authentic flavor:

1.  **Prepare the Lentils:** Begin by thoroughly rinsing the red lentils under cold running water until the water runs clear. This removes any impurities and excess starch, which can make the soup cloudy. Drain well.

2.  **Sauté the Aromatics:** In a large pot or Dutch oven, melt butter (or heat olive oil) over medium heat. Add the finely diced onion and sauté until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant, being careful not to burn it.

3.  **Build the Flavor Base:** Stir in the tomato paste and cook for 2-3 minutes, stirring constantly. This step, known as "cooking out" the tomato paste, deepens its flavor and removes any raw taste. Add the dried mint and red pepper flakes, stirring for about 30 seconds until fragrant.

4.  **Add Lentils and Liquid:** Add the rinsed and drained red lentils to the pot. Pour in the water or broth. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are very tender and have started to break down. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.

5.  **Blend to Perfection:** Once the lentils are tender, remove the pot from the heat. Use an immersion blender to blend the soup directly in the pot until it is completely smooth and creamy. If you don't have an immersion blender, carefully transfer the soup in batches to a regular blender (filling only halfway and venting the lid) and blend until smooth, then return it to the pot.

6.  **Adjust Consistency and Seasoning:** If the soup is too thick, add a little more hot water or broth until it reaches your desired consistency. Season generously with salt and black pepper to taste. Simmer for another 5 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.

7.  **Prepare the Nane Yagi (Mint Butter Topping):** While the soup is simmering, melt a knob of butter in a small pan over medium-low heat. Once melted, add a generous pinch of dried mint and a sprinkle of red pepper flakes. Sauté for about 30 seconds until fragrant, being careful not to burn the mint. This step is crucial for authentic flavor!

8.  **Serve:** Ladle the hot soup into bowls. Drizzle each serving with a spoonful of the fragrant mint butter topping and serve immediately with fresh lemon wedges on the side. A squeeze of fresh lemon juice just before eating is non-negotiable!

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

To truly master this Turkish Red Lentil Soup and elevate it from good to extraordinary, keep these professional tips in mind:

*   **Rinse Lentils Thoroughly:** This is critical. Rinsing removes starch that can make the soup gummy or cloudy. Keep rinsing until the water is clear.
*   **Cook Out the Tomato Paste:** Don't rush this step. Allowing the tomato paste to cook for a few minutes with the aromatics deepens its flavor significantly and removes any metallic taste.
*   **Don't Skimp on Dried Mint:** Dried mint is a signature flavor in Mercimek Corbasi. Use good quality dried mint and don't be afraid to be generous with it, especially in the tempered butter topping.
*   **Blend Until Silky Smooth:** The characteristic texture of Mercimek Corbasi is its velvety smoothness. Blend thoroughly, ensuring no chunks of lentil remain. A fine mesh sieve can be used for an ultra-silky finish, though it's often not necessary with a good blender.
*   **The Power of Lemon:** Always, always serve with fresh lemon wedges. A squeeze of fresh lemon juice at the table brightens all the flavors, adds a crucial tang, and cuts through the richness of the lentils. It's the secret ingredient that ties everything together.
*   **Tempered Butter (Nane Yagi):** The mint-infused butter topping is not just a garnish; it's a flavor bomb. It adds richness, aroma, and a beautiful visual appeal. Don't skip it!
*   **Adjust Consistency:** Lentils absorb liquid, so the soup can thicken as it sits. Feel free to thin it out with a little hot water or broth when reheating or if you prefer a thinner consistency.

## Serving Suggestions

Serving a bowl of this fragrant Turkish Red Lentil Soup is an experience in itself. While it’s utterly satisfying on its own, here are some traditional and delicious ways to enjoy it:

*   **With Fresh Lemon:** As mentioned, a squeeze of fresh lemon juice is non-negotiable. It brightens the flavors and adds a crucial tang.
*   **Crusty Bread:** A generous slice of crusty bread, such as Turkish pide or simit, is perfect for scooping up every last drop of the creamy soup. Even a simple baguette or pita bread works wonderfully.
*   **Shepherd's Salad (Çoban Salatasi):** A refreshing, finely diced salad of tomatoes, cucumbers, onions, and peppers, dressed with olive oil and lemon, provides a lovely contrast to the warm, hearty soup.
*   **Pickles (Tursu):** A side of assorted Turkish pickles offers a delightful sour and briny counterpoint.
*   **Spicy Kick:** For those who love a bit of heat, a sprinkle of extra red pepper flakes or a drizzle of chili oil can be added at the table.
*   **As a Starter:** Mercimek Corbasi traditionally serves as a comforting appetizer before a larger meal.
*   **Light Meal:** Paired with bread and a salad, it makes for a perfectly balanced and fulfilling light lunch or dinner.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Turkish Red Lentil Soup recipe is perfect as is, there are a few ways you can adapt it to suit your preferences or dietary needs:

*   **Vegan Version:** This soup is already naturally vegan if you use olive oil instead of butter for sautéing the aromatics and for the topping. Ensure your broth is vegetable-based.
*   **Add Vegetables:** While not traditional for the classic creamy version, some variations might include finely diced carrots or potatoes added with the lentils to cook, contributing extra nutrients and a slightly different texture. They would also blend smoothly.
*   **Smoky Flavor:** A pinch of smoked paprika can add a subtle smoky depth to the soup, complementing the earthy lentils.
*   **Spice Level:** Easily adjust the amount of red pepper flakes (pul biber) to make it as mild or as spicy as you prefer. A little cayenne pepper can also be used for extra heat.
*   **Herb Variations:** While dried mint is king, a tiny pinch of fresh parsley or cilantro added at the very end as a garnish can offer a fresh twist, though it deviates from the traditional flavor profile.
*   **Thicker or Thinner:** Adjust the amount of liquid to achieve your desired consistency. For a thicker soup, reduce the liquid; for a thinner one, add more hot water or broth.
*   **Lentil Type:** While red lentils are standard, you can experiment with a small portion of yellow split peas for a slightly different flavor and texture, though it will change the soup's characteristic color.

Mercimek Corbasi is more than just a soup; it's a warm embrace in a bowl, a testament to the power of simple ingredients transformed into something extraordinary. This Turkish Red Lentil Soup offers a comforting taste of Turkish hospitality, inviting you to savor every spoonful. Whether you're new to Turkish cuisine or a seasoned fan, this Mercimek Corbasi is a must-try, promising a deeply satisfying and flavorful experience that you'll want to recreate again and again.
